heading: Transfer of Government-owned long-lines communication facilities in and to Alaska
label: 17702 Transfer of Government-owned long-lines communication facilities in and to Alaska
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)17702(a)(1)40:781(1)., , .Pub. children.heading: Authority of the secretary of defense.—
children.label: 1 Authority of the secretary of defense.—

children.heading: .—Requirements prior to transfer
children.label: A .—Requirements prior to transfer
children.text: Subject to and with the advice, assistance, and, in the case of an agency not under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense, the consent of the agency concerned, and after approval of the President, the Secretary of Defense shall transfer for adequate consideration any or all long-lines communication facilities in or to Alaska under the jurisdiction of the Federal Government to any person qualifying under section 17703.section 17703 of this title

children.heading: .—Authority to carry out chapter
children.label: B .—Authority to carry out chapter
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may take action and exercise powers as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Consent of secretary concerned
children.label: 2 .—Consent of secretary concerned
children.text: An interest in public lands, withdrawn or otherwise appropriated, shall not be transferred under this chapter without the prior consent of the Secretary of the Interior, or, with respect to lands in a national forest, of the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: .—Procedures and methods
children.label: 3 .—Procedures and methods
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall carry out a transfer under this chapter in accordance with the procedures and methods required of the Administrator of General Services by section 545(a) and (b) of this title.

children.heading: .—Documents of Title or Other Property Interests
children.label: b .—Documents of Title or Other Property Interests
children.text: The head of the agency concerned (or a designee of the head) shall execute documents for the transfer of title or other interest in property, except any mineral rights in the property, and take other action that the Secretary of Defense decides is necessary or proper to transfer the property under this chapter. A copy of a deed, lease, or other instrument executed by or on behalf of the head of the agency concerned purporting to transfer title or another interest in public land shall be provided to the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: .—Solicitation of Offers To Purchase Certain Facilities
children.label: c .—Solicitation of Offers To Purchase Certain Facilities
B
children.text: the amounts considered to be the current fair and reasonable value of those facilities; and

children.label: C
children.text: the initial rates that will be charged to the purchaser for capacity in facilities retained by the Government and available for commercial use;
provide in the request for offers to purchase that offerors must specify the rates the offerors propose to charge for service and the improvements in service the offerors propose to initiate;

provide an opportunity for prospective purchasers to meet as a group with Department of Defense representatives to ensure that the data and public interest requirements described in clauses (1) and (2) are fully understood; and

seek the advice and assistance of the Federal Communications Commission and the Governor of Alaska (or a designee of the Governor) to ensure consideration of all public interest factors associated with the transfer.

children.heading: .—Applicability of Antitrust Provisions
children.label: d .—Applicability of Antitrust Provisions
children.text: The requirements of apply to transfers under this chapter.section 559 of this title
